title: Mandevilla oaxacensis
intro: Mandevilla oaxacensis is a climbing evergreen vine native to Mexico, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including diabetes, hypertension, and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits in regulating blood sugar levels and blood pressure, as well as its anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Mandevilla oaxacensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The most common herbal preparations made from Mandevilla oaxacensis include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions and as a dietary supplement to promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Mandevilla oaxacensis is classified within the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family consists of flowering plants that are native to the Americas, Africa, and Asia. Mandevilla oaxacensis is a species of flowering plant that is native to Mexico.
